seoTitle: How to Create an Event Registration Page in Squarespace
metaDescription: Build an event registration page in Squarespace with custom branding, payment integration, and forms to manage attendees smoothly.
intro: Managing event attendees becomes much easier with a dedicated registration page that handles sign-ups, payments, and confirmations in one place. This guide covers how to build one in Squarespace, from page layout to payment integration, so your event runs smoothly from the start. It's built for event organizers, non-profits, and small businesses hosting virtual or in-person events.

steps.heading: Set up your Squarespace site
steps.body: Start with the page or section of your site where the event registration page will live.

steps.heading: Create the event registration page
steps.body: Build out the page layout following the step-by-step instructions, tailoring it to your event's details.

steps.heading: Customize the registration form
steps.body: Adjust the form fields and template design to match your brand and collect the information you need from attendees.

steps.heading: Integrate payment options
steps.body: Set up payment solutions within the page so ticketed events can process transactions seamlessly.

steps.heading: Automate attendee management
steps.body: Explore automation options for follow-ups and confirmations to make attendee communication easier and more consistent.

faq.question: Can I collect payments through my event registration page?
faq.answer: Yes, the tutorial covers integrating payment solutions so attendees can pay for tickets directly through the registration page.

faq.question: Is this suitable for free events?
faq.answer: Yes, the registration page can be customized to collect attendee information whether or not the event requires payment.

faq.question: Can I automate confirmation emails for attendees?
faq.answer: The tutorial explores automation possibilities to simplify follow-ups and confirmations, making attendee engagement easier to manage.

faq.question: Who is this tutorial best suited for?
faq.answer: Event organizers, small business owners, educators, and anyone hosting virtual or in-person events will find this useful.
